DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAR-26-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE METZGER'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO SUBPOENA OF BUSINESS RECORDS FROM KELVIN BROWN AND ASSOCIATES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THIRD PARTY KELVIN BROWN HAS NOW FULLY COMPLIED WITH THE SUBPOENA. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. KELVIN BROWN IS A NON-PARTY WHICH WAS IN PRO PER UNTIL AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED. IT LARGELY COMPLIED WITH THE SUBPOENA BEFORE THE MOTION WAS FILED OTHER THAN NOT SIGNING AN AUTHENTICITY DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY AND SOME UNDERSTANDABLE CONFUSION IN THE PRODUCTION. THE COURT BELIEVES THAT IT WOULD NOT BE IN THE INTERESTS OF JUSTICE TO SANCTION KELVIN BROWN. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM BRUCE.HIGHMAN@HIGHMANLAW.COM. MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT LAWRENCE METZGER's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT:

Cross-defendant Metzger's motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to TDG's prayer for attorney's fees. TDG fails to cite a contractual, statutory, or legal basis for the relief and does not explain how the defect can be cured by amendment. The motion to strike the claim for setoff is granted without leave to amend. The relief should be sought in the answer. "We hold that a defendant may not obtain an award of affirmative relief against a plaintiff by way of section 431.70; rather, the defendant may only assert the setoff defensively to defeat the plaintiff's claim in whole or in part." Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 198. The remainder of the motion is denied. Those challenged allegations provide background and explain how Metzger has allegedly breached his duties and continues to do so. The allegations are not necessarily time barred based on CCP Sec 431.70 and they are not "improper" or "irrelevant" within the meaning of CCP Sec 436(a).

Counsel for the cross-defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
